**Evidence**: - Mandibular second premolars typically erupt slightly before second molars in the natural progression. - This alignment supports proper occlusal relationships without requiring intervention.

Other options involve deviations from established timelines (e.g., canines preceding premolars) that may indicate anomalies warranting further evaluation. Thus, **C remains the most relevant choice**, confirming its status as having "little or no significance."
